_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Spinal AVM embolization - Using Histoacryl (per sitting)
min_doctor_qualification: MCh/DNB/Equivalent in (Neurosurgery, if required Vascular Surgeon)
clinical_indications: Spinal arteriovenous malformations (AVMs) representing 3%-4% of all space-occupying lesions affecting the spinal cord
Progressive neurological symptoms over months to years (e.g., back pain, progressive sensory loss, lower extremity weakness)
Sudden onset of weakness, numbness, difficulty urinating, urinary incontinence, fecal incontinence, or paralysis (especially in patients younger than 30) due to hemorrhage
Lesions that can be treated via minimally invasive endovascular embolization of the AVM to obliterate it
Therapeutic option as part of a multidisciplinary approach, depending on the hemodynamics of the lesion, its site and angioarchitecture

preauth.label: Clinical notes including neurological evaluation findings and planned line of treatment
Complete Blood Count, Creatinine, Prothrombin time (PT)/ INR

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of treatment?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and line of treatment?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was the imaging indicative of surgery?
Is the discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Clinical presentation, severity, or imaging not indicative of surgery.
Patient has evidence of unfavourable anatomy, renal insufficiency, contrast allergy, or non-correctable coagulopathy.
Clinical notes lack detailed history, signs & symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, or planned line of treatment.
Imaging did not confirm the diagnosis.
Embolization material invoice/barcode not submitted with claim.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) are missing daily vitals or line of treatment.

clinical_key_pointers: Spinal arteriovenous malformations (AVMs) are abnormal collections of blood vessels in the spinal canal with a direct connection between arterial and venous systems without intervening capillaries.
Spinal dural AVMs are the most common type in adults.
Intradural AVMs are located outside the substance of the spinal cord, and Intramedullary AVMs are located within the substance of the spinal cord.
Classification includes Type I (dural AVF), Type II (glomus intramedullary AVM), Type III (juvenile malformations), and Type IV (perimedullary AVF).

Symptoms include progressive neurological symptoms (back pain, sensory loss, lower extremity weakness) in 85% of cases, and sudden onset of weakness, numbness, difficulty urinating, incontinence, or paralysis (due to hemorrhage) in 10-20% of cases.
Treatment involves careful consideration of clinical history, symptoms, physical examination, and diagnostic studies. A multidisciplinary approach is essential for planning management.
